Method Name: National adaptation strategy and legally binding targets
Description / Application: Sets long-term national priorities and legal commitments to reduce climate risks across sectors.

Method Name: Integration of climate risk into policy appraisal
Description / Application: Ensures all public investment and policy decisions account for current and future climate risks.
Defines place-based actions by local governments to manage climate impacts and improve resilience.
Coordinates emergency services and agencies to respond effectively to extreme weather events.
Provides shared access to consistent climate data for decision-making across organisations.
Creates virtual replicas of systems to test climate impacts and adaptation options.
Maps spatial climate risks such as flooding or heat to support planning and intervention targeting.
Uses data and tools to evaluate impacts after climate events and inform recovery.

Equivalent Project: Urban Catchment Partnerships / England Trees Action Plan
Description / Application: Increases tree cover in cities to reduce heat, manage water, and improve air quality. Planting trees to sequester carbon, provide shade, and manage water.

Equivalent Project: Medmerry Managed Realignment / Nature Recovery Network
Description / Application: Restores wetlands to store floodwater, improve biodiversity, and enhance natural resilience. Creating new wetland areas to store water and support wildlife.
Rewets degraded peatlands to reduce emissions, store water, and limit wildfire risk.
Using natural landscape features to slow water flow and reduce flood risk. Reconnects rivers to their natural floodplains to reduce downstream flood risk.

Equivalent Project: Green Infrastructure Framework / SuDS Planning Advice
Description / Application: Increasing vegetation in urban areas to combat the urban heat island effect. Installs vegetated roofs to absorb rainfall, reduce heat, and improve urban microclimates.
Repairs natural habitats damaged by flooding to restore ecosystem services.
Large-scale infrastructure to protect against river and coastal flooding. Physical barriers designed to prevent floodwaters from inundating land and property.

Equivalent Project: Capital Flood Defence Schemes / Thames Barrier / Boston Barrier
Description / Application: Large-scale infrastructure to protect against river and coastal flooding. Large-scale infrastructure to control sea surges and protect coastal areas.
Increases water storage capacity to manage drought and supply variability.

Reconstructs infrastructure and buildings to improved resilience standards after damage.

Preventing sewage backflow into properties during flood events.
Restricting new construction in areas at high risk of flooding.
Forecasting and alerting the public about impending extreme weather.

Moving water from areas of surplus to areas of deficit.
Collecting and storing rainwater for non-potable uses.

Campaigns to encourage the public to use less water.

Connecting fragmented habitats to allow species migration.
Improving soil health to increase water retention and prevent erosion.
Strategies to prevent and control wildfires, including controlled burns.

Practices like no-till farming to prevent soil degradation.

Conducting exercises to prepare for severe climate impact scenarios.
Educating and equipping communities to respond to local climate risks.
